/* Support */ rm entire WP:OR section. Neither of the "Time" sources mention Bhardwaj's "theory", so the suggestion that "This theory was confirmed in 2014" is entirely WP:SYNTHESIS. Without this connection, Bhardwaj's essay does not meet the notability bar. The linked software that he developed is also apparently spyware.

/* Support */ the source says AT&T lobbied *against* net neutrality. the project was stopped because it would have been unprofitable in the event of a new net neutrality regulation. this move does not belong under "support".

/* Criticism */ Removal of "Computer Science Experts" from the introductory sentence claiming them as opponents. Review of the citations does not support this claim that "Computer Science experts" oppose Net Neutrality regulations. "Computer Science experts" is a large, diverse group of individual professionals with individual opinions and there is not sufficient evidence to suggest that Computer Scientists, in general and as a group, oppose net neutrality.
